fields.name: TAMPE
fields.description: Tamper detection on INx enable

fields.name: ITAMPE
fields.description: Internal tamper X enable

fields.name: TAMPNOER
fields.description: Tamper X no erase

fields.name: TAMPMSK
fields.description: Tamper X mask. The tamper 1 interrupt must not be enabled when TAMP1MSK is set.

fields.name: BKBLOCK
fields.description: Backup registers and device secrets access blocked

fields.name: BKERASE
fields.description: Backup registers and device secrets erase. Writing '1 to this bit reset the backup registers and device secrets(1). Writing 0 has no effect. This bit is always read as 0.

fields.name: TAMPTRG
fields.description: Active level for tamper 1 input.

fields.name: ITAMPNOER
fields.description: Internal Tamper X no erase

fields.name: TAMPFREQ
fields.description: Tamper sampling frequency. Determines the frequency at which each of the INx inputs are sampled.

fields.name: TAMPFLT
fields.description: INx filter count. These bits determines the number of consecutive samples at the specified level (TAMP*TRG) needed to activate a tamper event. TAMPFLT is valid for each of the INx inputs.

fields.name: TAMPPRCH
fields.description: INx precharge duration. These bit determines the duration of time during which the pull-up/is activated before each sample. TAMPPRCH is valid for each of the INx inputs.

fields.name: TAMPPUDIS
fields.description: INx pull-up disable. This bit determines if each of the TAMPx pins are precharged before each sample.

fields.name: TAMPAM
fields.description: Tamper X active mode

fields.name: ATOSEL
fields.description: Active tamper shared output X selection. The selected output must be available in the package pinout

fields.name: ATCKSEL
fields.description: Active tamper RTC asynchronous prescaler clock selection. These bits selects the RTC asynchronous prescaler stage output.The selected clock is CK_ATPRE.. fCK_ATPRE = fRTCCLK / 2ATCKSEL when (PREDIV_A+1) = 128.. .... These bits can be written only when all active tampers are disabled. The write protection remains for up to 1.5 ck_atpre cycles after all the active tampers are disable.

fields.name: ATPER
fields.description: Active tamper output change period. The tamper output is changed every CK_ATPER = (2ATPER x CK_ATPRE) cycles. Refer to .

fields.name: ATOSHARE
fields.description: Active tamper output sharing. IN1 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL1. IN2 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL2. IN3 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL3. IN4 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL4. IN5 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL5. IN6 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL6. IN7 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL7. IN8 is compared with TAMPOUTSEL8

fields.name: FLTEN
fields.description: Active tamper filter enable

fields.name: SEED
fields.description: Pseudo-random generator seed value. This register must be written four times with 32-bit values to provide the 128-bit seed to the PRNG. Writing to this register automatically sends the seed value to the PRNG.

fields.name: PRNG
fields.description: Pseudo-random generator value. This field provides the values of the PRNG output. Because of potential inconsistencies due to synchronization delays, PRNG must be read at least twice. The read value is correct if it is equal to previous read value. This field can only be read when the APB is in secure mode.

fields.name: SEEDF
fields.description: Seed running flag. This flag is set by hardware when a new seed is written in the ATSEEDR. It is cleared by hardware when the PRNG has absorbed this new seed, and by system reset. The TAMP APB cock must not be switched off as long as SEEDF is set.

fields.name: INITS
fields.description: Active tamper initialization status. This flag is set by hardware when the PRNG has absorbed the first 128-bit seed, meaning that the enabled active tampers are functional. This flag is cleared when the active tampers are disabled.

fields.name: ATOSEL
fields.description: Active tamper shared output X selection. The selected output must be available in the package pinout. Bits 9:8 are the mirror of ATOSEL1[1:0] in the ATCR1, and so can also be read or. written through ATCR1.

fields.name: BKPRWSEC
fields.description: Backup registers read/write protection offset. Protection zone 1 is defined for backup registers from BKP0R to BKPxR (x = BKPRWSEC-1, from 0 to 128). if TZEN=1, these backup registers can be read and written only with secure access. If TZEN=0:	the protection zone 1 can be read and written with non-secure access. If BKPRWSEC = 0: there is no protection zone 1. If BKPRWPRIV is set, BKPRWSEC[7:0] can be written only in privileged mode.

fields.name: CNT1SEC
fields.description: Monotonic counter 1 secure protection

fields.name: BKPWSEC
fields.description: Backup registers write protection offset. Protection zone 2 is defined for backup registers from BKPyR (y = BKPRWSEC, from 0 to 128) to BKPzR (z = BKPWSEC-1, from 0 to 128, BKPWSECBKPRWSEC): if TZEN=1, these backup registers can be written only with secure access. They can be read with secure or non-secure access. Protection zone 3 defined for backup registers from BKPtR (t = BKPWSEC, from 0 to 127). They can be read or written with secure or non-secure access. If TZEN=0:	the protection zone 2 can be read and written with non-secure access. If BKPWSEC = 0 or if BKPWSEC BKPRWSEC: there is no protection zone 2. If BKPWPRIV is set, BKPRWSEC[7:0] can be written only in privileged mode.

fields.name: BHKLOCK
fields.description: Boot hardware key lock. This bit can be read and can only be written to 1 by software. It is cleared by hardware together with the backup registers following a tamper detection event or when the readout protection (RDP) is disabled.

fields.name: TAMPSEC
fields.description: Tamper protection (excluding monotonic counters and backup registers). Note: Refer to for details on the read protection.

fields.name: CNT1PRIV
fields.description: Monotonic counter 1 privilege protection

fields.name: BKPRWPRIV
fields.description: Backup registers zone 1 privilege protection

fields.name: BKPWPRIV
fields.description: Backup registers zone 2 privilege protection

fields.name: TAMPPRIV
fields.description: Tamper privilege protection (excluding backup registers). Note: Refer to for details on the read protection.

fields.name: TAMPIE
fields.description: Tamper X interrupt enable

fields.name: ITAMPIE
fields.description: Internal tamper X interrupt enable

fields.name: TAMPF
fields.description: TAMPx detection flag. This flag is set by hardware when a tamper detection event is detected on the TAMPx input.

fields.name: ITAMPF
fields.description: Internal tamper X flag. This flag is set by hardware when a tamper detection event is detected on the internal tamper X.

registers.name: MISR
registers.description: TAMP non-secure masked interrupt status register
registers.fieldset_description: TAMP non-secure masked interrupt status register

fields.name: TAMPMF
fields.description: TAMPx non-secure interrupt masked flag. This flag is set by hardware when the tamper X non-secure interrupt is raised.

fields.name: ITAMPMF
fields.description: Internal tamper X non-secure interrupt masked flag. This flag is set by hardware when the internal tamper X non-secure interrupt is raised.

registers.name: SMISR
registers.description: TAMP secure masked interrupt status register
registers.fieldset_description: TAMP secure masked interrupt status register

fields.name: TAMPMF
fields.description: TAMPx secure interrupt masked flag. This flag is set by hardware when the tamper X secure interrupt is raised.

fields.name: ITAMPMF
fields.description: Internal tamper X secure interrupt masked flag. This flag is set by hardware when the internal tamper X secure interrupt is raised.

fields.name: CTAMPF
fields.description: Clear TAMPx detection flag. Writing 1 in this bit clears the TAMPxF bit in the SR register.

fields.name: CITAMPF
fields.description: Clear ITAMPx detection flag. Writing 1 in this bit clears the ITAMPxF bit in the SR register.

fields.name: COUNT
fields.description: This register is read-only only and is incremented by one when a write access is done to this register. This register cannot roll-over and is frozen when reaching the maximum value.

fields.name: ERCFG0
fields.description: Configurable device secrets configuration

fields.name: BKP
fields.description: The application can write or read data to and from these registers. In the default (ERASE) configuration this register is reset on a tamper detection event. It is forced to reset value as long as there is at least one internal or external tamper flag being set. This register is also reset when the readout protection (RDP) is disabled.

variants.name: Div2
variants.description: RTCCLK/2 is selected when (PREDIV_A+1) = 128 (actually selects 1st flip flop output)

variants.name: Div4
variants.description: RTCCLK/4 is selected when (PREDIV_A+1) = 128 (actually selects 2nd flip flop output)

variants.name: Div128
variants.description: RTCCLK/128 is selected when (PREDIV_A+1) = 128 (actually selects 7th flip flop output)

variants.name: NoFilter
variants.description: Tamper event is activated on edge of INx input transitions to the active level (no internal pull-up on INx input).

variants.name: Filter2
variants.description: Tamper event is activated after 2 consecutive samples at the active level.

variants.name: Filter4
variants.description: Tamper event is activated after 4 consecutive samples at the active level.

variants.name: Filter8
variants.description: Tamper event is activated after 8 consecutive samples at the active level.

variants.name: FilteredLowOrUnfilteredHigh
variants.description: If TAMPFLT 00 Tamper 2 input staying low triggers a tamper detection event.

variants.name: FilteredHighOrUnfilteredLow
variants.description: If TAMPFLT 00 Tamper 2 input staying high triggers a tamper detection event.
